title: rudbeckia
intro: Rudbeckia, also known as the coneflower or black-eyed Susan, is a perennial plant native to North America, characterized by its bright yellow or orange petals with a dark center. The roots and leaves of the rudbeckia plant have been used in traditional medicine for centuries, particularly in Native American and European folk remedies, due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving digestion, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and antiviral activity. Herbal preparations of rudbeckia, such as teas and infusions, are sometimes used to treat various ailments, including sore throats and skin conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
